rationale: Ran `go test ./internal/instruct/...`: only the pre-documented z4ps `TestRun_IdleNoBeads` failure (confirmed identical on parent e32eeaed via inspection of the old file — same multi-active-spec cwd trip, unrelated to this diff); the two pinned Allow-reason rows and the new 14-row `TestPanelStateVerdict_DelegatesToPanelGateDecision` table pass. `go build ./...` clean. Read gate.go's PanelGateDecision and panelstate.go's verdict()/gatherPanelState side by side: every decision-surface case (fresh, stale-SHA, round-mismatch, malformed x2, incomplete, REJECT, hard_block, sub/at/above threshold, abandoned, missing-ref, non-bead) maps correctly; Allow-reason synthesis reuses the exact pinned wording, Warn/Block relay `Decision.Message` verbatim. Non-bead panels correctly leave HeadSHA/BeadID zero, keeping staleness inert. On the deliberate nil-Panel change: traced Scan->load and the fresh Tally(reg.Dir) inside ResolveGateFacts — confirmed the old code (pre-refactor, read from /tmp) had the identical Scan-then-Tally TOCTOU window and would fail-open Allow on it; the new Block is not newly-introduced risk, and `internal/complete` already routes real merges through this same PanelGateDecision malformed branch, so this change actually REMOVES a pre-existing divergence between the advisory preview and the real gate rather than creating a new one. Doc-sync line is accurate and cites the real ADR-0040. `internal/panel` untouched (confirmed via diff --stat).
findings: Confirmed empirically (not just by BRIEF assertion) that TestRun_IdleNoBeads is pre-existing: read the old panelstate.go's verdict() (git show 25ad5ca9^) and gatherPanelState — the same panel.Scan()-then-panel.Tally(reg.Dir) two-step existed before this bead, so the nil-Panel/nil-PanelErr TOCTOU window (panel.json deleted between the two reads) is not new; only the OUTCOME on that rare race changed (old: fail-open Allow "no panel.json — unregistered"; new: fail-closed Block "unreadable").

That outcome change is in fact a convergence fix, not a new risk: internal/complete/panel_advisory.go + complete.go already call the same panel.ResolveGateFacts/panel.PanelGateDecision for the real merge gate, so the malformed-registration Block was already what `mindspec complete` would do for real on this race; the old instruct copy was the one that disagreed. This bead removes that disagreement, consistent with ADR-0040's stated purpose.

The delegation-parity test's `factsFor` test helper duplicates verdict()'s own fact-construction rather than being fully independent, so its main power comes from the paired source-text assertions (must call panel.PanelGateDecision(, must not contain the 4 deleted literals) — worth noting but not a defect; the two together are sufficient to catch a regressed local branch.
Verified ADR-0040 (.mindspec/adr/ADR-0040-orchestration-layering-ratchet.md) exists and the doc-sync paragraph in .mindspec/domains/workflow/overview.md accurately describes the change.
git diff 25ad5ca9^ 25ad5ca9 --stat -- internal/panel/ is empty — the shared panel package is genuinely untouched, honoring the scope fence.
